questions: Does the implementation satisfy all 5 acceptance criteria from the ticket?
Are there any regressions to the success-path output?
Does the error handling meet the ticket's failure behavior requirements?
Are there any correctness issues, missed edge cases, or code quality concerns?

answers.answer: Yes. All 5 acceptance criteria are satisfied: (AC1) Empty-result path prints run ID and follow-up suggestion via the combined block at lines 55-77. (AC2) User-supplied --run value is echoed exactly — line 57 initializes resolvedRunId from the getFlag result and the resolution fetch at lines 59-73 only runs when runId is undefined. (AC3) Success path is unchanged — the combined check only triggers when both items and stepArtifactSummary are empty, and the existing output blocks at lines 32-53 are structurally identical to the pre-change code. (AC4) Zero-runs ticket prints 'No runs available for this ticket.' at line 66 with no follow-up. (AC5) Resolution failure catch block at lines 69-72 prints a graceful note and returns normally.
evidence: src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 55-77: combined empty-result block with all required branches
src/tickets/artifacts.ts line 57: let resolvedRunId = runId (user-supplied value preserved)
src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 69-72: catch block with graceful message, no throw/process.exit

answers.answer: No regressions. The existing output blocks (items display at lines 32-43, step artifact summary at lines 45-53) are structurally untouched. The combined empty block at lines 55-77 only executes when both arrays are empty, so it never appends to non-empty output. The existing follow-up suggestion on line 50 (for non-empty stepArtifactSummary) is preserved.
evidence: src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 32-43: items display block identical to original
src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 45-53: stepArtifactSummary block identical to original
src/tickets/artifacts.ts line 56: guard condition data.items.length === 0 && data.stepArtifactSummary.length === 0

answers.answer: Yes. The try/catch around the ticket-detail fetch (lines 60-72) catches any error from hxFetch (which throws after 3 retry attempts per src/lib/http.ts), prints a single-line graceful message, and returns normally. There is no throw, no process.exit(), and no retry in the catch block. The 'No artifacts found.' message is already printed by line 42 before the combined block runs, satisfying the requirement that it appears even on resolution failure.
evidence: src/tickets/artifacts.ts lines 69-72: catch block prints message and returns
src/tickets/artifacts.ts line 42: 'No artifacts found.' prints before combined block
src/lib/http.ts lines 70-133: hxFetch throws Error after retries

answers.answer: No correctness issues or code quality concerns found. The implementation follows the established run-ID resolution pattern from artifact.ts (lines 5-10, 29-40) and bundle.ts (lines 7-13, 33-39). Local type declarations follow the codebase convention of type duplication. The getFlag return type (string | undefined) matches the resolvedRunId declaration. The hxFetch call for ticket detail uses the same basePath and path pattern as sibling commands. Edge cases (empty string from getFlag is not possible per the getFlag implementation which requires a next argument) are handled correctly.
evidence: src/tickets/artifact.ts lines 5-10, 29-40: reference pattern for types and resolution
src/tickets/bundle.ts lines 7-13, 33-39: duplicate pattern confirming convention
src/lib/flags.ts lines 5-9: getFlag returns undefined when flag missing or no value

statement: Code review complete. The single-file change to src/tickets/artifacts.ts correctly implements all 5 acceptance criteria. The combined empty-result block at lines 55-77 resolves run IDs following the established codebase pattern, handles all edge cases (user-supplied --run, no runs, resolution failure), and preserves the success-path output unchanged. TypeScript compilation and all 30 existing tests pass clean. No issues found; no code changes made by review.
